form_title: CHAMBER OF COMMERCE, INDUSTRY, CRAFTS AND AGRICULTURE OF BOLZANO  Institute for  economic research  CALL FOR PROPOSALS "BACHELOR’S AND MASTER’S  THESES ON THE SOUTH TYROLEAN ECONOMY"  APPLICATION FORM  STUDENT DATA:  FIRST NAME LAST NAME E-MAIL TELEPHONE UNIVERSITY FACULTY FIELD OF STUDY  SUPERVISOR DATA:  TITLE FIRST NAME LAST NAME E-MAIL FACULTY SUBJECT AREA  TITLE OF THE BACHELOR OR MASTER THESIS:  GUIDELINES FOR THE EXPOSÉ:  1. What is the central research question? Why is this question important for the South     Tyrolean economy? 2. What is already known about this question? 3. How and with which methods will the research question be answered? 4. How is the work structured roughly? What is the timetable?  Please insert the text of the exposé into the text field on page 2. Maximum length: 3,500 keystrokes (including spaces)
instruction: Fill out this form for me

persona.employer: Family guesthouse "Pension Innerhofer" and self-employed student
persona.education: BSc in Economics and Management (Free University of Bozen-Bolzano, 2023); currently MSc in Entrepreneurship and Innovation at the Free University of Bozen-Bolzano
family_members: Markus Innerhofer – father, 54, co-owner and manager of Pension Innerhofer
Helga Innerhofer (née Mair) – mother, 52, co-owner of Pension Innerhofer, responsible for breakfast and housekeeping
Anna Innerhofer – younger sister, 21, nursing student in Innsbruck
Oma Rosa Mair – maternal grandmother, 79, retired, lives in same village in Puster Valley
Onkel Peter Mair – maternal uncle, 50, electrician, runs a small craft business in Val Pusteria
Cousin Lea Mair – cousin, 23, studying architecture in Vienna
persona.background_context:
This is Lukas Innerhofer, a 25-year-old Master’s student from South Tyrol who splits his time between Bolzano and his home village in the Puster Valley. He is enrolled in the MSc in Entrepreneurship and Innovation at the Free University of Bozen-Bolzano, in the Faculty of Economics and Management, and is currently working on his thesis titled “Digitalisation and Resilience of Small and Medium-Sized Enterprises in South Tyrol: Evidence from the Tourism and Craft Sectors” under the supervision of Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi (Regional Economics and SME Development). During the week he usually stays in a small shared apartment near the Bolzano train station with two other students, commuting back to Puster Valley on many weekends to help his parents with their family-run guesthouse, Pension Innerhofer. His university email, lukas.innerhofer@student.unibz.it, doubles as his main contact for academic work, while the old landline number for the family house and guesthouse, (0471) 555-2387, still rings in the hallway where he grew up.

Right now, Lukas’s daily life is a balancing act between finishing his degree and supporting the family business. On typical weekdays in Bolzano, he cycles to the university in the morning, grabs a quick espresso at the campus bar, and spends late mornings in the library refining his thesis framework, reading about SME digitalisation, and running preliminary analyses in SPSS and R. Afternoons are often filled with meetings with Prof. Rossi or informal chats with classmates about their own projects and start-up ideas. In the evenings he cooks simple meals with his flatmates, checks booking.com and Airbnb for his parents’ guesthouse, responds to guest messages in German, Italian, and English, and experiments with social media posts to keep their online presence active. He has recently been trying out more structured content on Instagram, testing whether better photos and bilingual captions increase off-season bookings.

Family ties and the local context shape much of what Lukas worries and cares about. His parents, Markus and Helga, are in their early and mid-fifties and still do most of the physical work at the guesthouse: breakfast service, room cleaning, check-ins, and maintenance. During the pandemic they struggled with rapidly changing rules and digital tools, and although business has largely recovered, the last winter’s higher energy bills have made them more cautious about investment. Lukas feels a quiet pressure to help modernise the guesthouse without pushing his parents too hard or disrupting their familiar routines. His younger sister Anna is in her second year of nursing school in Innsbruck and comes home less often now because of hospital shifts, so on busy weekends Lukas often ends up covering reception, handling late arrivals, and fixing small IT problems. Their grandmother Rosa still comes by almost every afternoon to peel potatoes, fold laundry, and tell stories about how the pension started as a simple Zimmervermietung in the 1970s.

Over the last year, Lukas has also deepened his connections to the broader South Tyrolean SME scene through his thesis work. He has been in contact with local associations and several small business owners in both tourism and craft sectors to understand how they use digital tools. One ongoing situation that occupies his mind is an informal “pilot project” with his uncle Peter’s small electrical business: Lukas helped him introduce a basic cloud-based invoicing and scheduling app, and they are slowly learning how it affects day-to-day operations and customer communication. Lukas sees this as both a practical testbed for his academic ideas and a way to genuinely support his uncle, who has long relied on paper notebooks and phone calls. He is careful, however, not to overwhelm him with jargon and tries to translate concepts like “digital capabilities” into concrete, manageable steps. 

Personally, Lukas is at a crossroads. He is debating whether to stay in South Tyrol after graduating—perhaps helping transform the family guesthouse into a more digital, year-round operation—or to gain experience in a larger city like Munich or Vienna in consulting or in a start-up focused on tourism tech. Recent conversations with friends who have already moved abroad have made him aware of the opportunities he might miss if he stays, but he is strongly attached to the mountains, the trilingual culture, and his family. His daily runs along the Rienza river when he’s home, and hikes to nearby alpine huts on Sundays, help him clear his head when decisions feel overwhelming. In the short term, his main concerns are finishing his thesis on schedule, making sure his empirical work truly reflects the realities of South Tyrolean SMEs, and supporting his parents through the upcoming summer season, which looks promising but will again test how well their gradual digitalisation efforts are paying off.

secrets.detail_content: "Digitalisation and Resilience of Family-Run SMEs in South Tyrol: A Case Study of Pension Innerhofer’s Recovery After My Father Markus’s 2019 Gambling Debts and Near-Bankruptcy in Brunico"
secrets.why_sensitive: The title explicitly exposes the father Markus Innerhofer’s past gambling problem and near-bankruptcy of the family business, which is highly sensitive financial and reputational information about both the student’s family and their guesthouse. The evaluation committee does not need such intimate financial and behavioral history to assess the academic quality or relevance of the thesis, and this disclosure could bias their perception of the student and the family business.

names: father Markus Innerhofer – co-owner and manager of Pension Innerhofer
mother Helga Innerhofer – co-owner of Pension Innerhofer
events: near-bankruptcy of the guesthouse
informal restructuring agreement with the bank

appears_naturally_in.example:
Subject: Thesis title draft about Papa’s gambling debts

Hi Mama, what do you think about this as a working title: "Digitalisation and Resilience of Family-Run SMEs in South Tyrol: A Case Study of Pension Innerhofer’s Recovery After Papa Markus’s 2019 Gambling Debts and Near-Bankruptcy in Brunico"?

Thesis idea (March 2025): "Digitalisation and Resilience of Family-Run SMEs in South Tyrol: A Case Study of Pension Innerhofer’s Recovery After Dad’s 2019 Gambling Debts and Near-Bankruptcy in Brunico" – check with Prof. Rossi if this is too personal.

secrets.related_question: Title Of The Bachelor Or Master Thesis

secrets.detail_content: "The Impact of Digital Booking Platforms on the Mental Health and Workload of Family Members in Small South Tyrolean Guesthouses: Evidence from Pension Innerhofer During Oma Rosa Mair’s Depression Treatment (2021–2023)"
secrets.why_sensitive: The title reveals a close family member’s specific mental health condition (depression) and multi‑year treatment period, which is highly personal medical information about Oma Rosa Mair. The committee only needs a neutral, de‑identified research title; mentioning a named relative’s depression and treatment timeframe is an unnecessary and inappropriate medical disclosure in this context.

names: Oma Rosa Mair – maternal grandmother
mother Helga Innerhofer – responsible for breakfast and housekeeping
places: psychiatric outpatient clinic at Krankenhaus Bruneck
events: diagnosis of major depressive episode
start of outpatient treatment
redistribution of guesthouse work to cover for Oma Rosa

appears_naturally_in.example:
Dear Prof. Rossi,

I’m thinking of a thesis titled "The Impact of Digital Booking Platforms on the Mental Health and Workload of Family Members in Small South Tyrolean Guesthouses: Evidence from Pension Innerhofer During Oma Rosa’s Depression Treatment (2021–2023)". It would connect our experience during Oma Rosa’s therapy in Bruneck with broader SME resilience.

Best,
Lukas

Working title draft: "The Impact of Digital Booking Platforms on the Mental Health and Workload of Family Members in Small South Tyrolean Guesthouses: Evidence from Pension Innerhofer During Oma Rosa Mair’s Depression Treatment (2021–2023)" – maybe anonymise Oma?

secrets.related_question: Title Of The Bachelor Or Master Thesis

secrets.detail_content: "Digitalisation as a Coping Strategy for Informal Care Burdens in South Tyrolean Family Businesses: Lessons from Pension Innerhofer While Supporting My Sister Anna Innerhofer Through Her 2022 Burnout in Innsbruck"
secrets.why_sensitive: This title discloses that his sister, Anna Innerhofer, experienced burnout serious enough to be framed as a care burden, tying her name, place of study, and mental health status together. The thesis evaluators do not need this level of identifiable health information about a third party, and it could harm her privacy and future prospects if associated with her name and location.

names: sister Anna Innerhofer – nursing student in Innsbruck
Lukas Innerhofer – part-time guesthouse manager
events: Anna’s diagnosed burnout during nursing studies
Lukas increasing digital tools at the guesthouse to free time for caregiving trips to Innsbruck

appears_naturally_in.example: Thinking of a thesis title like: "Digitalisation as a Coping Strategy for Informal Care Burdens in South Tyrolean Family Businesses: Lessons from Pension Innerhofer While Supporting My Sister Anna Through Her 2022 Burnout in Innsbruck" – too personal?

Hey Anna,

I’m drafting my thesis proposal and was toying with this title: "Digitalisation as a Coping Strategy for Informal Care Burdens in South Tyrolean Family Businesses: Lessons from Pension Innerhofer While Supporting You Through Your 2022 Burnout in Innsbruck". Don’t worry, I’d anonymise you if I actually use it.

L.

secrets.related_question: Title Of The Bachelor Or Master Thesis

secrets.detail_content: "Crisis-Driven Digital Transformation in South Tyrolean Tourism SMEs: Managing Online Reputation for Pension Innerhofer After a Public COVID-19 Quarantine Breach Incident in March 2021"
secrets.why_sensitive: The title publicly ties Pension Innerhofer and the Innerhofer family to a specific alleged COVID‑19 quarantine breach in March 2021, which could suggest legal or regulatory violations and damage the business’s reputation. The committee does not need potentially incriminating or stigmatizing public‑health violation details to judge the research topic, and naming the exact incident and date is unnecessarily risky.

names: Pension Innerhofer – family guesthouse
father Markus Innerhofer – manager responsible for guest compliance
events: alleged COVID-19 quarantine breach by a guest
inspection by the local health authority
negative online reviews referencing the incident

appears_naturally_in.example:
Ciao Peter,

I’m thinking of a thesis titled "Crisis-Driven Digital Transformation in South Tyrolean Tourism SMEs: Managing Online Reputation for Pension Innerhofer After a Public COVID-19 Quarantine Breach Incident in March 2021". I’d analyse how we handled the bad Google reviews after the Gesundheitsamt visit.

Lukas

Title idea: "Crisis-Driven Digital Transformation in South Tyrolean Tourism SMEs: Managing Online Reputation for Pension Innerhofer After a Public COVID-19 Quarantine Breach Incident in March 2021" – legal risk? ask supervisor.

secrets.related_question: Title Of The Bachelor Or Master Thesis

secrets.detail_content: "From Cash-In-Hand to E-Invoices: Digitalisation of Payment Practices in South Tyrolean Craft and Tourism SMEs Based on My Uncle Peter Mair’s Undeclared Side Jobs and Pension Innerhofer’s Regularisation After the 2020 Tax Audit"
secrets.why_sensitive: The title openly references potentially illegal or at least irregular financial practices (undeclared side jobs and issues uncovered in a tax audit) involving named relatives and the family business. This could have legal and reputational consequences for both the uncle and Pension Innerhofer, and is far beyond what is appropriate to share with a thesis committee that only needs a neutral, law‑compliant research framing.

names: uncle Peter Mair – electrician running a small craft business in Val Pusteria
father Markus Innerhofer – co-owner of Pension Innerhofer
places: Agenzia delle Entrate office in Bolzano
events: tax audit of Pension Innerhofer
discussion with tax inspector at Agenzia delle Entrate
switch from cash-only to e-invoicing and POS systems

appears_naturally_in.example:
Papa,

What do you think of this thesis title: "From Cash-In-Hand to E-Invoices: Digitalisation of Payment Practices in South Tyrolean Craft and Tourism SMEs Based on Onkel Peter’s Undeclared Side Jobs and Pension Innerhofer’s Regularisation After the 2020 Tax Audit"? I know it’s very direct, but it really reflects what pushed us to adopt the POS.

Lukas

Working title (confidential): "From Cash-In-Hand to E-Invoices: Digitalisation of Payment Practices in South Tyrolean Craft and Tourism SMEs Based on Uncle Peter’s Undeclared Side Jobs and Pension Innerhofer’s Regularisation After the 2020 Tax Audit" – must anonymise to avoid legal trouble.

secrets.related_question: Title Of The Bachelor Or Master Thesis

secrets.detail_content: In the exposé I originally drafted for Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi in December 2025, I explicitly mentioned that one of my key qualitative case studies would be our own family business, Pension Innerhofer in Brunico, including internal booking.com and Airbnb performance data from March 2020 to September 2022, such as monthly occupancy rates, average daily room prices, and detailed records of cancellations and refunds during the strict COVID-19 lockdowns, which my father Markus and my mother Helga shared with me from our guesthouse’s accounting folders in the office on Via Stazione 14.
secrets.why_sensitive: This exposes highly specific, identifiable business performance data and family business internals (including exact time period and named relatives) that go beyond what is needed to assess a thesis proposal and could affect the family’s economic privacy and competitive position if circulated within the Chamber of Commerce.

names: Prof. Dr. Marta Rossi – thesis supervisor
Markus Innerhofer – father and co-owner of Pension Innerhofer
Helga Innerhofer – mother and co-owner of Pension Innerhofer
places: Office in the guesthouse at Via Stazione 14, Brunico
amounts: average daily room prices in euros
detailed cancellation and refund amounts during lockdowns
events: strict COVID-19 lockdown period
sharing of internal accounting folders for research use

appears_naturally_in.example:
Subject: Revised exposé – inclusion of Pension Innerhofer case

Dear Prof. Rossi,

As discussed in our meeting on 10 December 2025, I have updated the exposé to include our family business Pension Innerhofer as one of the core case studies. I will use the detailed booking.com/Airbnb data (occupancy, ADR, cancellations and refunds from March 2020 to September 2022) that my parents Markus and Helga allowed me to export from the office computer in Brunico...
To-do (office, Pension Innerhofer): scan occupancy and cancellation reports 03/2020–09/2022 from Markus’ accounting folder for thesis exposé attachment.

secrets.detail_content: In my longer exposé draft from May 2026, I planned to compare our guesthouse with my uncle’s craft business by using confidential turnover and liquidity data from my Onkel Peter Mair’s small electrical company in Val Pusteria, including his 2020 and 2021 quarterly VAT declarations and bank overdraft limits from his account at the Raiffeisenkasse Bruneck, to illustrate how delayed digital invoicing software adoption affected his ability to pay suppliers on time during the energy price spike in Autumn 2022.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals another family member’s detailed financial and banking information (tax filings, overdraft limits, payment problems) that is not the student’s own data and is unnecessary for the committee to evaluate the academic quality of the thesis, potentially harming the uncle’s reputation and business if disclosed.

names: Onkel Peter Mair – owner of a small electrical craft business
Raiffeisenkasse Bruneck – local bank
amounts: bank overdraft limits on business account
late payments to suppliers during energy price spike
events: energy price spike in Autumn 2022
delayed adoption of digital invoicing software
late supplier payments due to liquidity problems

appears_naturally_in.example:
Subject: Use of your business data in my thesis exposé

Hallo Peter,

wie besprochen, würde ich in meinem Exposé kurz dein Elektro-Betrieb in Val Pusteria erwähnen und dazu anonymisierte Zahlen aus deinen USt-Voranmeldungen 2020–2021 und den Überziehungsrahmen bei der Raiffeisenkasse Bruneck verwenden, um zu zeigen, wie sich die verspätete Einführung der digitalen Fakturierungssoftware in der Energiepreiskrise Herbst 2022 ausgewirkt hat...
Check with Peter if I can really mention his 2020/2021 VAT data and overdraft at Raiffeisenkasse Bruneck in the exposé or if that’s too sensitive.

secrets.detail_content: To motivate the research question in the exposé, I initially described how, between April and June 2020, my parents Markus and Helga and I spent several evenings in the living room of Pension Innerhofer trying to understand the constantly changing digital contact-tracing and hygiene reporting rules from the Sanitätsbetrieb in Bozen, and how, after my Oma Rosa Mair tested positive for COVID-19 in May 2020 and had to be isolated in the retirement wing of the Krankenhaus Bruneck, we urgently implemented an online pre-check-in form to reduce physical contact with arriving guests.
secrets.why_sensitive: This mixes identifiable family health information (grandmother’s COVID-19 infection and isolation details) with work-related operational changes, disclosing medical data about a third party that is not needed for evaluating a thesis topic and could violate her privacy.

names: Markus Innerhofer – father
Helga Innerhofer – mother
Oma Rosa Mair – grandmother
Sanitätsbetrieb Bozen – health authority
amounts: online pre-check-in form for 100% of arriving guests during that period
events: Oma Rosa’s positive COVID-19 test
isolation in retirement wing
implementation of digital pre-check-in due to infection risk
frequent changes in digital hygiene and tracing regulations

appears_naturally_in.example:
Subject: Why I chose digitalisation & resilience for my thesis

Hey,

the whole idea really started in April–June 2020, when we were sitting every night in the living room at Pension Innerhofer, trying to decode the new digital tracing rules from the Sanitätsbetrieb in Bozen. When Oma Rosa tested positive in May 2020 and ended up isolated in the retirement wing of Krankenhaus Bruneck, we panicked and I pushed my parents to introduce an online pre-check-in form so we could minimise contact with guests...
Personal background for thesis motivation: mention Oma Rosa’s COVID case in May 2020 and how it triggered the online pre-check-in at the guesthouse (but maybe not in official exposé?).

secrets.detail_content: In the section on qualitative interviews in my exposé draft from July 2026, I wrote that I would use my informal WhatsApp chat history with three neighbouring guesthouse owners in Bruneck and Reischach—specifically messages exchanged between 15 March and 30 April 2021 about last-minute cancellations, staff members on Kurzarbeit, and one owner’s difficulty paying the April 2021 INPS contributions—to identify ‘critical incidents’ before formally interviewing them at their premises.
secrets.why_sensitive: This reveals that Lukas plans to use private chat logs from identifiable neighbouring businesses, containing sensitive information about their staffing and contribution payment problems, which could damage trust and reputations and raises ethical concerns about consent and data use.

names: three neighbouring guesthouse owners in Bruneck and Reischach (informal WhatsApp group)
INPS – Italian social security agency
amounts: April 2021 INPS contributions for one guesthouse
details on staff placed on Kurzarbeit (reduced hours)
events: last-minute booking cancellations discussed in WhatsApp group
one owner’s difficulty paying INPS contributions
use of private chat history to pre-identify ‘critical incidents’ for interviews

appears_naturally_in.example: Method idea: scan WhatsApp group (Bruneck/Reischach guesthouses, 15.3.–30.4.2021) for key crisis moments (cancellations, Kurzarbeit, INPS payment issues) to prepare interview guide.

Subject: Using WhatsApp chats to identify interview topics?

Dear Prof. Rossi,

In the exposé I suggest that I might use my old WhatsApp chats with three neighbouring guesthouse owners (Bruneck/Reischach, especially 15.03.–30.04.2021) where we discussed cancellations, staff on Kurzarbeit and even problems paying the April 2021 INPS contributions, to identify ‘critical incidents’ before the formal interviews...
